source.title: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 90 cohort studies of social isolation, loneliness and mortality.

confidence_rationale: Most recent and largest meta-analysis available (90 cohorts, 2,205,199 individuals), with PROSPERO pre-registration (CRD42022299959) and stratified outcomes by cause (all-cause, CVD, cancer). Replicates and extends 2010/2015 findings: social isolation HR=1.32 all-cause, 1.34 CVD, 1.24 cancer; loneliness HR=1.14 all-cause, 1.09 cancer. Effects also present in subpopulations with existing CVD or breast cancer, suggesting deprivation operates over and above pre-existing morbidity. Directly anchors the empirical premise behind Q-029.
failure_signatures: Loneliness effect size (HR=1.14) is notably smaller than isolation (HR=1.32), and the 2023 estimate for loneliness is smaller than the 2015 Holt-Lunstad pooled estimate (OR=1.26). Either the broader literature has dragged the loneliness effect down (publication bias correction or larger denominator), or the additional contribution of subjective unshared suffering above objective isolation is genuinely modest. Q-029's claim that loneliness is a distinct harm category becomes harder to defend if its independent contribution is marginal.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Across 90 prospective cohort studies covering 2.2 million individuals, social isolation predicts increased all-cause mortality (HR=1.32), CVD mortality (HR=1.34), and cancer mortality (HR=1.24); loneliness predicts increased all-cause (HR=1.14) and cancer mortality (HR=1.09). Effects replicate within CVD and breast cancer subpopulations.
mapping.ree_translation: This is the strongest currently available empirical anchor for Q-029's premise that absence of shared-world relations carries substrate-level cost. The cause-specific stratification is informative for the REE framing: deprivation harm is not just a generic frailty story but tracks with specific physiological mechanisms (cardiovascular regulation, immune function in cancer outcomes) that REE-style accounts can in principle map to mechanistic predictions about distributed regulation across an agent's substrate when its relational coupling is severed.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The systematic review still cannot dissociate the active mechanism. It rules out reverse causation more carefully (prospective design, subgroup with existing disease) but does not rule in any specific physiological pathway from loneliness to mortality. Q-029's architectural claim about REE substrate is an inference about why this happens, which the paper's design cannot adjudicate. Loneliness HR=1.14 is the smallest cleanly attributable effect.
mapping.source_context: Adult human cohorts including general population and disease-specific subgroups (CVD, breast cancer); prospective design; PROSPERO-registered; effect sizes reported as hazard ratios.

confidence_components.notes: Highest source_quality of the three meta-analytic entries (largest N, most recent, registered, cause-stratified). Mapping_fidelity moderate because the paper does not engage Q-029's ethical framing. Transfer_risk in line with the other meta-analyses.
